name: Seafood Lasagne
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the haddock and cod fillets in a pan. Pour over 20 fluid ounces of milk and add bay leaf.
2. Slowly bring the liquid to a boil, then simmer for 3 to 5 minutes until the fish is cooked. Remove the fish and strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into another container. Make up the remaining 30 fluid ounces with an additional 10 fluid ounces of milk.
3. In a separate pan, melt butter or margarine over low heat. Gradually stir in flour and gradually blend in milk until smooth. Return the pan to heat and continue stirring until the sauce thickens. Stir in half the cheese, parsley, and cayenne pepper. Season to taste.
4. Skin and flake the fish, then add prawns, eggs, and sweetcorn (plus any liquid from the can) into a bowl with the remaining ingredients except for lasagne sheets.
5. Spoon two-thirds of the sauce over the base of an ovenproof dish. Layer with lasagne sheets, repeating layers until the dish is full.
6. Preheat the oven to 180C/350F/Gas Mark 4. Pour any reserved sauce over the lasagne and mix together the remaining cheese and parsley. Sprinkle this mixture on top of the lasagne.
7. Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour, or until golden brown. Serve with salad.
